game_name: King's Quest: Quest for the Crown

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Obtained as key quest item; becomes series staple for viewing distant locations and receiving guidance
Must be destroyed as one of three main objectives; represents early dangerous encounters
Collected through exploration and puzzle-solving; demonstrates inventory-based progression
Items found and stored for later use; primary puzzle-solving methodology
Requires mapping and spatial reasoning; typical obstacle type
Logic and lateral thinking required; integrated with NPC encounters

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven with episodic tension; relief comes from puzzle completion and inventory accumulation; player paces their own progression through open-ended quest structure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Open exploration encourages finding alternate paths and hidden items; not all treasures require specific routes
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden areas accessible through experimentation; rewards thorough exploration of all screen spaces

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction through exploration; basic puzzle types teach inventory mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased complexity in maze navigation and riddle-solving; antagonist encounters escalate
difficulty_curve.late_game: Culmination with witch destruction; synthesis of all learned mechanics and collected items
notable_spikes: First encounter with Dahlia or her agents

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple objectives (three treasures + witch) can be pursued in varying orders; player autonomy in route selection
Maze-like areas requiring spatial mapping and logical navigation; typical adventure game challenge type
Access to new areas and puzzle solutions gated by inventory items found earlier; encourages backtracking and revisiting spaces
Characters present riddles and logic puzzles; information-gathering mechanic integral to progression
